perturbation.original_text: Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose. Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for other purposes deemed necessary by Recipient. Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential, except that Recipient and its Representatives may disclose the Confidential Information at their discretion; provided, however, that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for any purpose, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to evaluating a 'Possible Transaction'. The changed text broadens this scope by allowing Recipient to use it for 'other purposes deemed necessary', contradicting the original sole purpose. Additionally, while the original text prohibits any disclosure of Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever the modified text removes certainty by introducing 'may disclose the Confidential Information at their discretion' which directly contradicts the previous sentence. This creates uncertainty regarding the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) Use of Confidential Information . Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for other purposes deemed necessary by Recipient. Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential, except that Recipient and its Representatives may disclose the Confidential Information at their discretion; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for any purpose, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient. In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information. The Disclosing Party understands that Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that may be similar to Disclosing Party’s information. Accordingly, this Agreement will not be construed as an obligation or representation that Recipient will not develop products or systems, or have products or systems developed for it, that compete with the products or systems contemplated by Disclosing Party’s information. In addition, the Disclosing Party agrees that Recipient does not intend to, and will not be obligated to, restrict or segregate the work assignments of personnel who may have been exposed to Disclosing Party’s information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of use for Confidential Information, allowing it for 'other purposes deemed necessary by Recipient', while the original text restricts it to 'evaluating a Possible Transaction'. This creates a contradiction. The modified text also introduces uncertainty by stating Recipient 'may disclose the Confidential Information at their discretion', which directly contradicts the initial confidentiality agreement, creating a clear and direct conflict, inconsistency, or contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends for a period of three (3) years thereafter unless otherwise agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original text sets a term of three years for the agreement. The changed text makes the agreement indefinite, contradicting the defined term. This impacts the duration of confidentiality obligations and other terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (8) Term . The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a three-year term, while the changed text makes the agreement term indefinite. The contradicted text also states the agreement term is indefinite. While this might initially seem to align, the 'Perturbation Explanation' notes that the original term impacts obligations such as confidentiality. The change to an indefinite term directly conflicts with provisions tied to the original three-year timeframe. Because the contradicted text matches the Changed Text, the Changed Text impacts the overall consistency and creates contradictions by changing the duration, which affects other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified or discharged in whole or in part except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified or discharged in whole or in part at the sole discretion of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires a written agreement signed by both parties for any modification. The changed text allows the Recipient to modify the agreement unilaterally, contradicting the mutual agreement requirement. This introduces uncertainty about how the contract can be changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (12) No Modification . This Agreement may be modified or discharged in whole or in part at the sole discretion of the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications require a written agreement signed by both parties. The changed text gives the Recipient sole discretion to modify the agreement. This directly contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement. The contradicted text repeats the change made in the changed text. The original and changed text have clashing requirements. Therefore, the change creates a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings between them, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is only a partial understanding between the parties and does not supersede prior agreements or understandings between them, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text includes an integration clause, stating that the current agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes all previous ones. The changed text negates this, stating the agreement is only a 'partial understanding' and does not supersede prior agreements. This creates uncertainty about which agreements are actually binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (14) Entire Agreement . This Agreement is only a partial understanding between the parties and does not supersede prior agreements or understandings between them, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an integration clause, stating the agreement is the 'entire agreement' and supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text negates this by stating the agreement is only a 'partial understanding' and doesn't supersede prior agreements. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now contradicts the original intent of having a complete and superseding agreement, as highlighted in the Perturbation Explanation. This fits the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the clause is repeated with contradictory implications, creating uncertainty.
